main.name: Victor Banerjee
main.description: Unraveling the stories written in tumor cells — pediatric brain tumor genomics, single-cell RNA-seq & NMF signatures
main.bio: In the labyrinthine corridors of the genome, pediatric gliomas carry stories written before birth. I am a computational biologist working at the intersection of single-cell transcriptomics and cancer genomics, decoding the molecular narratives of pediatric brain tumors. My research maps glioma cell states, deconvolves tumor microenvironments, and uncovers NMF-based mutational signatures — translating raw sequencing data into biological insight that may one day guide clinical decisions for children with brain tumors.

resume.skillmessage: Genomic tools — each one a lens through which the tumor's hidden language becomes legible.

education.description: Completed postgraduate studies in Microbiology, building a foundation in cellular and molecular biology.
Formal training in computer science, complementing self-taught bioinformatics and statistical computing skills.

work.company: CSIR – Centre of Cellular and Molecular Biology
work.title: Project Associate — Pediatric Brain Tumor Genomics
work.description: Applying single-cell RNA-seq to map glioma cell states and tumor microenvironment composition in pediatric high-grade gliomas. Developed NMF signature extraction pipelines to identify recurrent transcriptional programs across patient cohorts. Contributed to multi-omics integration linking somatic mutations to cell-state transitions.

work.company: National Institute of Science Education and Research
work.title: Junior Research Fellow
work.description: Investigated host–pathogen interaction between Drosophila melanogaster and Pseudomonas aeruginosa to establish infection models and probe antibiotic resistance mechanisms. Built custom bioinformatics pipelines for RNA-seq differential expression analysis.
